purpose: Identifies pricing inconsistencies across equity, credit, and futures surfaces for the same underlying commodity to reveal genuine uncertainty or mispricing
components: Equity-implied commodity price: reverse-engineer the price where mining equity valuations become consistent with other surfaces
Forward curve shape: backwardation/contango signals about physical tightness
Credit spread level: distress or complacency signals for commodity-sector borrowers
Inconsistency diagnosis: mutually contradictory pricing across surfaces implies at least one must reprice
current_assessment: Copper pricing surfaces are internally inconsistent. Equities imply $9.5-10k/t (moderate tightness), not the $12k+ required by the structural deficit thesis. Forward curve and credit spreads are both benign. The inconsistency reveals the market has not reached consensus on deficit magnitude.
limitations: Equity valuations reflect operating leverage, management quality, and jurisdiction risk beyond commodity price
Credit spreads may lag physical market developments due to covenant structures
Forward curve shape affected by financialization dynamics (contango bias from index rebalancing)
Surface reconciliation may require several quarters of data to resolve
